gpt55_review_summary: GPT-5.5 found that the first legacy facade only re-exported the curated document __all__, which would break old direct imports and star imports for non-curated names such as ENGINE_ADAPTER_HANDOFF_RECORD_TYPE and IN_PROCESS_PAYLOAD_SOURCE. The facade now copies all non-private names from document_kv_cache.engine_adapters while omitting legacy __all__, and regression tests cover direct imports plus star-import behavior. The follow-up GPT-5.5 review approved the slice.
verification: poetry run pytest tests/test_engine_adapters.py tests/test_public_package.py tests/test_project_governance.py -q
repository secret scan over README.md, CONTRIBUTING.md, pyproject.toml, src, tests, databricks, pr-evidence, and .github
what_changed: Moved the engine-adapter implementation into document_kv_cache.engine_adapters with document-owned imports and curated document __all__.
Replaced restaurant_kv_serving.engine_adapters with a compatibility facade that exposes all non-private document module names without defining a legacy __all__.
Added document-root exports and tests for adapter module ownership, legacy direct imports, legacy star imports, and public package compatibility.
why: The vLLM/SGLang adapter handoff contract is a core document-kv-cache serving boundary, so it must be owned by the document namespace while preserving existing restaurant import compatibility during migration.
